property.name: java.library.path

testcase.name: runScenario["Get All Products List - TC1", "TC1 - Get All Products List"](1)
testcase.system-out: @TC1 Scenario: Get All Products List - TC1 # src/test/resources/features/TC1_GetAllProducts.feature:11 Given the API endpoint is available for products # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_api_endpoint_is_available_for_products() When I make a GET request to "/api/productsList" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC1_GetAllProductsSteps.i_make_a_get_request_to(java.lang.String) Then the response status code should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_status_code_should_be(int) And the response should contain a valid products list # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_should_contain_a_valid_products_list() And the response JSON schema should be valid for products list # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_json_schema_should_be_valid_for_products_list() And the response code in body should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_code_in_body_should_be(int)

testcase.name: runScenario["Get Single Product Details by Search - TC2", "TC2 - Get Single Product Details"](2)
testcase.system-out: @TC2 Scenario: Get Single Product Details by Search - TC2 # src/test/resources/features/TC2_GetSingleProduct.feature:11 Given the API endpoint is available for products # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_api_endpoint_is_available_for_products() When I make a POST request to "/api/searchProduct" with search parameter "top" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.i_make_a_post_request_to_with_search_parameter(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) Then the response status code should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_status_code_should_be(int) And the response should contain searched products # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC2_GetSingleProductSteps.the_response_should_contain_searched_products() And the response JSON schema should be valid for search results # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_json_schema_should_be_valid_for_search_results() And the response code in body should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_code_in_body_should_be(int)

testcase.name: runScenario["Get Single Product Details with Different Search Terms - TC2", "TC2 - Get Single Product Details"](3)
testcase.system-out: @TC2 Scenario Outline: Get Single Product Details with Different Search Terms - TC2 # src/test/resources/features/TC2_GetSingleProduct.feature:28 Given the API endpoint is available for products # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_api_endpoint_is_available_for_products() When I make a POST request to "/api/searchProduct" with search parameter "top" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.i_make_a_post_request_to_with_search_parameter(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) Then the response status code should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_status_code_should_be(int) And the response should contain searched products for "top" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC2_GetSingleProductSteps.the_response_should_contain_searched_products_for(java.lang.String) And the response JSON schema should be valid for search results # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_json_schema_should_be_valid_for_search_results() And the response code in body should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_code_in_body_should_be(int)

testcase.name: runScenario["Get Single Product Details with Different Search Terms - TC2", "TC2 - Get Single Product Details"](4)
testcase.system-out: @TC2 Scenario Outline: Get Single Product Details with Different Search Terms - TC2 # src/test/resources/features/TC2_GetSingleProduct.feature:29 Given the API endpoint is available for products # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_api_endpoint_is_available_for_products() When I make a POST request to "/api/searchProduct" with search parameter "tshirt" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.i_make_a_post_request_to_with_search_parameter(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) Then the response status code should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_status_code_should_be(int) And the response should contain searched products for "tshirt" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC2_GetSingleProductSteps.the_response_should_contain_searched_products_for(java.lang.String) And the response JSON schema should be valid for search results # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_json_schema_should_be_valid_for_search_results() And the response code in body should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_code_in_body_should_be(int)

testcase.name: runScenario["Get Single Product Details with Different Search Terms - TC2", "TC2 - Get Single Product Details"](5)
testcase.system-out: @TC2 Scenario Outline: Get Single Product Details with Different Search Terms - TC2 # src/test/resources/features/TC2_GetSingleProduct.feature:30 Given the API endpoint is available for products # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_api_endpoint_is_available_for_products() When I make a POST request to "/api/searchProduct" with search parameter "jean" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.i_make_a_post_request_to_with_search_parameter(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) Then the response status code should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_status_code_should_be(int) And the response should contain searched products for "jean" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC2_GetSingleProductSteps.the_response_should_contain_searched_products_for(java.lang.String) And the response JSON schema should be valid for search results # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_json_schema_should_be_valid_for_search_results() And the response code in body should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_code_in_body_should_be(int)

testcase.name: runScenario["Create New User Account - TC3", "TC3 - Create User Account"](6)
testcase.system-out: @TC3 Scenario: Create New User Account - TC3 # src/test/resources/features/TC3_CreateUser.feature:11 Given the API endpoint is available for user account operations # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_api_endpoint_is_available_for_user_account_operations() When I make a POST request to "/api/createAccount" with valid user details from configuration # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC3_CreateUserSteps.i_make_a_post_request_to_with_valid_user_details_from_configuration(java.lang.String) Then the response status code should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_status_code_should_be(int) And the response message should be "Email already exists!" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_message_should_be(java.lang.String) And the response JSON schema should be valid for user creation # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC3_CreateUserSteps.the_response_json_schema_should_be_valid_for_user_creation() And the response code in body should be 400 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_code_in_body_should_be(int)

testcase.name: runScenario["Delete User Account - TC4", "TC4 - Delete User Account"](7)
testcase.system-out: @TC4 Scenario: Delete User Account - TC4 # src/test/resources/features/TC4_DeleteUser.feature:11 Given the API endpoint is available for user account operations # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_api_endpoint_is_available_for_user_account_operations() When I make a DELETE request to "/api/deleteAccount" with user credentials from configuration # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC4_DeleteUserSteps.i_make_a_delete_request_to_with_user_credentials_from_configuration(java.lang.String) Then the response status code should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_status_code_should_be(int) And the response message should be "Account not found!" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_message_should_be(java.lang.String) And the response JSON schema should be valid for account deletion # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC4_DeleteUserSteps.the_response_json_schema_should_be_valid_for_account_deletion() And the response code in body should be 404 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_code_in_body_should_be(int)

testcase.name: runScenario["Update User Account - TC5", "TC5 - Update User Account"](8)
testcase.system-out: @TC5 Scenario: Update User Account - TC5 # src/test/resources/features/TC5_UpdateUser.feature:11 Given the API endpoint is available for user account operations # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_api_endpoint_is_available_for_user_account_operations() When I make a PUT request to "/api/updateAccount" with updated user details from configuration # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC5_UpdateUserSteps.i_make_a_put_request_to_with_updated_user_details_from_configuration(java.lang.String) Then the response status code should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_status_code_should_be(int) And the response message should be "Account not found!" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_message_should_be(java.lang.String) And the response JSON schema should be valid for user update # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC5_UpdateUserSteps.the_response_json_schema_should_be_valid_for_user_update() And the response code in body should be 404 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_code_in_body_should_be(int)

testcase.name: runScenario["Negative Validation - Invalid Email - TC6", "TC6 - Negative Validation"](9)
testcase.system-out: @TC6 Scenario: Negative Validation - Invalid Email - TC6 # src/test/resources/features/TC6_NegativeValidation.feature:11 Given the API endpoint is available for user account operations # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_api_endpoint_is_available_for_user_account_operations() When I make a POST request to "/api/createAccount" with invalid email from configuration # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC6_NegativeValidationSteps.i_make_a_post_request_to_with_invalid_email_from_configuration(java.lang.String) Then the response status code should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_status_code_should_be(int) And the response should contain error message about invalid email # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC6_NegativeValidationSteps.the_response_should_contain_error_message_about_invalid_email() And the response JSON schema should be valid for error response # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_json_schema_should_be_valid_for_error_response()

testcase.name: runScenario["Negative Validation - Missing Required Fields - TC6", "TC6 - Negative Validation"](10)
testcase.system-out: @TC6 Scenario: Negative Validation - Missing Required Fields - TC6 # src/test/resources/features/TC6_NegativeValidation.feature:18 Given the API endpoint is available for user account operations # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_api_endpoint_is_available_for_user_account_operations() When I make a POST request to "/api/createAccount" with missing required fields # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC6_NegativeValidationSteps.i_make_a_post_request_to_with_missing_required_fields(java.lang.String,io.cucumber.datatable.DataTable) | name | email | password | title | birth_date | birth_month | birth_year | firstname | lastname | company | address1 | country | zipcode | state | city | mobile_number | | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | [empty] | Then the response status code should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_status_code_should_be(int) And the response should contain error message about email already exists # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC6_NegativeValidationSteps.the_response_should_contain_error_message_about_email_already_exists() And the response JSON schema should be valid for error response # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_json_schema_should_be_valid_for_error_response()

testcase.name: runScenario["Negative Validation - Invalid Search Parameter - TC6", "TC6 - Negative Validation"](11)
testcase.system-out: @TC6 Scenario: Negative Validation - Invalid Search Parameter - TC6 # src/test/resources/features/TC6_NegativeValidation.feature:27 Given the API endpoint is available for user account operations # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_api_endpoint_is_available_for_user_account_operations() When I make a POST request to "/api/searchProduct" with search parameter "nonexistentproduct123" # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.i_make_a_post_request_to_with_search_parameter(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) Then the response status code should be 200 # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_status_code_should_be(int) And the response should contain empty search results # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.TC6_NegativeValidationSteps.the_response_should_contain_empty_search_results() And the response JSON schema should be valid for search results # com.framework.api.stepdefinitions.CommonSteps.the_response_json_schema_should_be_valid_for_search_results()
